sourceMovie.title: Stalker
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Stalker works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Bicentennial Man
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stalker clicked for you, Bicentennial Man (1999) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Upside Down
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Stalker clicked for you, Upside Down (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Stalker?
faq.answer: Try Bicentennial Man, Upside Down, The Day the Earth Stood Still—each shares DNA with Stalker while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Stalker?
faq.answer: Stalker (1979) is best described as Science Fiction, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Stalker worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
